exemplar.title: A Replication of the Study by Cohen et al. (1981) to Investigate The Impact of Stereotypes on The Unreliability of Memory

detailedExemplarData.title: A Replication of the Study by Cohen et al. (1981) to Investigate The Impact of Stereotypes on The Unreliability of Memory

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear statement of aim with realâworld relevance
Accurate summary of schema theory and original study
Fully operationalized IV and DV with correctly stated hypotheses
weaknesses: Theoretical depth could be enhanced by comparing alternative memory distortion theories
Relevance examples, while concrete, are somewhat brief
Minor vagueness in describing how occupation labels instantiate the manipulation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction meets the top band in all strands but with minor lapses in depth and precision. The aim is clearly stated and its relevance identified (band 5), schema theory and the original study are accurately described and linked (band 5), and variables are fully operationalized with proper hypotheses (band 6). Taken together, this warrants a solid 5/6, reflecting strong performance with room for deeper theoretical exploration.

strengths: Well-justified independent-measures design demonstrating understanding of validity threats
Convenience sampling described with rationale and participant characteristics
Controlled variables and materials are identified and reasons given to minimize confounds
weaknesses: Potential sampling biases not critically assessed
No inter-rater reliability reported for coding procedures
Support for the efficacy of the distraction task is suggested but not cited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three exploration strands are explained rather than merely described, showing sound understanding of research design, sampling rationale, and control of variables/materials. Minor omissions in critical assessment of bias and reliability prevent full exemplary performance but justify the maximum 4/4 for this criterion.

strengths: Appropriate use of both descriptive and inferential statistics
Statistical procedures largely suitable to test the hypothesis
Graphical presentation relates to the hypothesis and includes error bars
weaknesses: Misalignment in focus of statistical analysis (schema-consistent vs inconsistent rather than by occupation group)
Claim of confidence intervals without calculation
Graph lacks full labeling and differentiation between conditions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis employs both descriptive and inferential statistics at an upper-band level (band 4) and presents a relevant graph (band 3â4), but conceptual misinterpretations and technical errors (misâfocused comparisons, missing confidence intervals, incomplete labels) limit this to 4/6. This score reflects competent use of statistics tempered by significant flaws in execution and interpretation.

strengths: Discussion of findings clearly linked to schema theory and original study
Strengths and limitations are not only stated but explained in terms of validity and generalizability
Modifications are explicitly connected to identified limitations
weaknesses: Depth of justification for modifications is limited
Some inconsistencies in the conclusion regarding statistical significance
Quantification of certain limitations (e.g., subtitle comprehension delays) is lacking
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates strong conceptual integration (band 4), comprehensive explanation of strengths and limitations (band 5), and relevant, linked modifications (band 3â4). The quality of the strengths/limitations discussion and theoretical linkage justifies a 5/6, recognizing that refinement of modification details and resolution of minor inconsistencies would be needed for perfection.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph with error bars is supplied and it relates to the hypothesis, yet axis labels are minimal and the comparison plotted does not match the stated IV conditions, constituting technical error.  Thus the product fits the 3-4 band but at its lower point.

Findings are summarised and linked back to accepting the alternative hypothesis and schema theory, but the misalignment in analytical focus plus a claim about confidence intervals not actually calculated show partial misinterpretation.  Hence mid-band (3).

Both IV (occupation label: librarian vs waitress) and DV (number of schema-consistent/inconsistent details recalled) are fully operationalised, and null plus directional research hypotheses are written correctly.  This fulfils the highest expectations; award the top of the band.

Sampling (convenience) is described and the rationale of time constraints plus participant characteristics is provided alongside recognition of cultural make-up.  That counts as an explanation rather than a simple description, so top band for this criterion.

Schema theory and the original Cohen study are summarised accurately and explicitly connected to the present investigationâs design (occupation label â schema driven recall).  The description is complete, though not deeply critical, so a solid 5 within the 5-6 band is appropriate.

The student discusses results in relation to schema theory and Cohen et al. with adequate depth, demonstrating conceptual integration.  Discussion is solid though not deeply critical, warranting the 3-4 band at its top.

The student clearly states the purpose of replicating Cohen et al. (1981) and then explains why examining stereotype-based memory errors matters in education, employment and legal settings.  These concrete examples go beyond merely identifying relevance, therefore the work matches the top-band descriptor.  Minor brevity in the real-world links keeps it at 5 rather than 6.

Several strengths (independent measures, standardisation) and limitations (convenience sample, cultural mix, subtitle issues) are each explained in terms of how they impact validity or generalisability, satisfying the âexplained and relevantâ requirement.  Depth is good but not exhaustive, so 5 within top band.

Key controlled variables (same video, tasks, timing, instructions) are listed with reasons showing how they reduce confounds.  Materials (translated video, distraction task) are also justified.  This meets the âexplainedâ descriptor.

Proposed modifications (use English video; larger, randomly selected, culturally homogeneous sample) are clearly linked to respective limitations, yet justification is brief and not fully developed (no specifics on how to implement or measure improvement).  This aligns with the 3-4 band but nearer its lower value.

The independent-measures design is not only described but justified against repeated measures, showing understanding of validity threats; hence an âexplainedâ level.  Minor omissions (e.g., no mention of random allocation procedure details) stop it from being exemplary but still merit the upper band.

Both descriptive and inferential statistics are employed and mostly appropriate (means, SD, Mann-Whitney).  However, the raw-data structure and comparison focus (schema-consistent vs inconsistent rather than between groups) indicate conceptual weakness, so the work sits at the upper end of the 3-4 band rather than being fully accurate.

annotations.comment: Strength: The title clearly identifies the original study and key variables, establishing context for the investigation.
Suggestion: Consider adding an abstract summarizing key methods and findings for clearer overview before the introduction.
Suggestion: Align the document structure with IA guidelines by clearly signposting abstract, methods, and results in the table of contents.
Suggestion: Move the "Introduction" heading and its opening paragraph onto the same page to improve flow and readability.
Suggestion: Provide a brief background before theory description to frame the investigation aim more effectively.
Suggestion: Ensure the aim of the investigation is clearly signposted in the introduction to meet IB requirements.
Strength: The definition of a stereotype from Cardwell (1996) provides a solid theoretical foundation.
Weakness: The link between schema theory and alternative memory distortion theories is not explored, limiting theoretical depth.
Suggestion: Complete the aim statement to include the distraction task and expected outcome in full, ensuring clarity and operationalization.
Strength: The student explains the real-world relevance of investigating stereotype-driven memory errors in education, employment, and legal contexts.
Weakness: Describing the independent variable simply as "stereotypes" is vague; specify precisely how the occupation labels serve as the manipulation.
Suggestion: Clarify categorization procedures and report inter-rater reliability for coding schema-consistent versus inconsistent details.
Strength: The justification for an independent measures design to avoid order effects demonstrates understanding of validity considerations.
Weakness: Convenience sampling is justified but the potential biases and lack of representativeness are not critically assessed.
Suggestion: Cite evidence or prior studies to support the effectiveness of chosen distraction tasks in disrupting immediate memory.
Strength: Controlled variables (instructions, video, task, timing) are well identified and justified to minimize confounding factors.
Weakness: Table 1 is empty and missing key values and labels; complete the table to present descriptive statistics clearly.
Strength: Table 2 accurately reports means for schema-consistent and inconsistent conditions, facilitating comparison.
Weakness: The graph lacks axis labels and fails to distinguish between occupation conditions, hindering its relevance to the stated hypothesis.
Weakness: The Mann-Whitney description mislabels the second p-value under the same condition; specify which condition each p value refers to.
Weakness: The text mentions a 95% confidence interval that is not calculated or presented; include interval computations or remove the claim.
Strength: The evaluation effectively links findings to schema theory and the original Cohen et al. study, demonstrating sound theoretical integration.
Strength: The student articulates strengths (design validity) and limitations (sample size), showing relevance to the investigationâs validity and generalizability.
Suggestion: Consider assessing cultural subgroup differences or adding a quantitative follow-up to gauge how cultural variability impacted recall.
Weakness: The subtitle translation issue is noted but not quantified; consider measuring comprehension delays induced by reading captions.
Weakness: The conclusion states findings were not statistically significant despite p<0.05 earlier; reconcile this inconsistency for logical coherence.
Suggestion: Include DOI or full publication details for Cohen et al. (1981) and other primary sources to adhere to APA citation standards.
